steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: In the expression $9yz^3 + 6yz + 3yz^3$, we identify terms with identical variable parts. The terms $9yz^3$ and $3yz^3$ both contain $yz^3$, making them like terms. The term $6yz$ contains only $yz$, so it is separate.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Add the coefficients of the like terms:
$$9yz^3 + 3yz^3 = 12yz^3$$
The complete simplified expression is:
$$12yz^3 + 6yz$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
